title: Post-Session Log
description: Feedback collected after the workout. This is the most valuable signal in the system — prescribed vs. actual divergence is how the rules engine calibrates over time.

session_plan_ref.description: Filename of the session plan this log corresponds to. e.g., '2026-03-14-session-plan.json'

actual_exercises.description: What actually happened. Same structure as session-plan exercises but with actual numbers. Omit exercises that were skipped and note why.

reps_completed.description: Actual reps, e.g. '10, 10, 9' for three sets

notes.description: Anything notable — form breakdown, easier than expected, had to stop early, etc.

overall_rpe.description: How hard did the session feel overall? 1 = barely moved, 10 = maximum effort, nothing left.

energy_after.description: How do you feel post-session? 1 = depleted/wiped, 5 = energized. Consistently scoring 1–2 suggests over-programming.

prediction_accuracy.description: Did the readiness assessment match how the session actually felt? Over-estimated = system thought you were more ready than you were. Under-estimated = system was too conservative.

prediction_accuracy_notes.description: Optional detail on why the prediction was off. e.g., 'HRV said HIGH but legs were toast from yesterday's run.'

post_workout_biometrics.description: Biometric readings taken immediately after the workout. The delta between pre-workout snapshot and these values is a direct measure of autonomic stress imposed by the session. Collected every session.

hr_at_stop_bpm.description: Heart rate at the moment of stopping the last exercise. Read from Apple Watch.

hr_1min_recovery_bpm.description: Heart rate 1 minute after stopping. The drop from hr_at_stop is Heart Rate Recovery (HRR). A drop of 20+ bpm in 1 min is normal for trained individuals. Declining HRR at the same workload over time is an early overtraining signal.

hrr_delta.description: Computed: hr_at_stop_bpm minus hr_1min_recovery_bpm. Higher = better parasympathetic reactivation. Do not ask the user for this — compute it.

next_morning_recovery.description: Optional. Biometrics from the morning after the session. Comparing these to the pre-workout snapshot reveals whether the body has recovered from the session. Most valuable signal for validating readiness predictions.

hrv_ms.description: Next-morning HRV from Apple Health. Compare to pre-workout HRV: a drop > 15% suggests the session imposed more stress than the readiness engine predicted.

rhr_bpm.description: Next-morning RHR. An elevation > 5bpm above pre-workout RHR suggests incomplete recovery.

notes.description: Anything else. How you're feeling, what felt good, what to adjust.

flag_for_rule_review.description: Set to true if this session produced a result that seems to contradict a system rule. Triggers a review prompt next session.

flag_reason.description: If flag_for_rule_review is true, explain what happened and which rule may need adjustment.
